issues.quote: Q4 2025 diluted EPS: $9.88 actual vs. consensus $8.3992 — a +5.7% positive surprise (surprise value $0.4808)
issues.reason: Wrong actual EPS. Per corpus/research/META/earnings.json, META's Q4 2025 (period 2025-12-31, year 2025 quarter 4) actual EPS was $8.88, not $9.88. The consensus $8.3992, surprise value $0.4808 and surprise % +5.7244% are all correct (and only consistent with the actual $8.88, since 8.3992 + 0.4808 = 8.88, not 9.88). The full-year EPS sum of 30.70 derived in the same paragraph is therefore also wrong; correct sum = 6.43 + 7.14 + 7.25 + 8.88 = 29.70. Both downstream figures stem from this single injected error.

issues.quote: Tesla's annual report (10-K) for fiscal year 2024 was filed with the SEC on February 5, 2025 (accession 0001628280-25-003063)
issues.reason: Wrong filing date. The 10-K with accession 0001628280-25-003063 was filed on 2025-01-30, not 2025-02-05. Source: corpus/filings/TSLA/10-K__2025-01-30__0001628280-25-003063.htm (filename encodes the filing date YYYY-MM-DD), and the same date appears in corpus/research/TSLA/sec_submissions.json for that accession.

issues.quote: Q1 FY26 ... $4.50 actual vs. consensus $4.65 — a -3.2% miss (surprise value -$0.15)
issues.reason: Sign and magnitude of the EPS surprise are wrong (consensus and miss/beat direction inverted). Per corpus/research/COST/earnings.json, the most recent quarter (period 2025-12-31, year 2026 quarter 1) reports actual $4.50, estimate $4.3571, surprise +0.1429, surprisePercent +3.2797 — i.e. a +3.3% BEAT, not a -3.2% miss. Consequently the report's narrative that this is the second consecutive miss is also factually wrong: only the prior FY25 Q4 print was a small miss (-0.8%); FY26 Q1 was a beat.

issues.quote: Walmart's fiscal year ends in late January, so its fiscal Q3 FY26 is the three months ending November 30, 2025
issues.reason: Wrong fiscal-quarter end date. Per corpus/research/WMT/financials_reported.json, the most recent 10-Q's FY2026Q3 entry has endDate 2025-10-31 (startDate 2025-02-01), i.e. Walmart's fiscal Q3 FY26 ends October 31, 2025, not November 30, 2025. (The Finnhub `earnings.json` `period` field for this quarter is the calendar-quarter-end label 2025-12-31, which is a separate Finnhub labeling convention and does NOT correspond to the WMT fiscal-quarter-end date.)

issues.quote: PFE's peer basket is LLY, JNJ, MRK, BMY, ZTS, RPRX, VTRS, ELAN, AXSM and NVS
issues.reason: Wrong peer membership. Per corpus/research/PFE/peers.json, the data array is exactly ['LLY', 'JNJ', 'MRK', 'PFE', 'BMY', 'ZTS', 'RPRX', 'VTRS', 'ELAN', 'AXSM'] — nine peers besides PFE itself: LLY, JNJ, MRK, BMY, ZTS, RPRX, VTRS, ELAN, AXSM. NVS (Novartis) is not in PFE's Finnhub peer list. The report appends NVS as the tenth name, which is factually wrong.

issues.quote: a calendar-year price decline of approximately -50.6% (close-to-close against $504.51 on 2025-01-02)
issues.reason: Wrong calendar-year return. Using the closes cited in the same report — 2025-01-02 close $504.51 and 2025-12-31 close $330.11 — the correct return is (330.11 / 504.51) - 1 ≈ -34.6%, not -50.6%. (To produce -50.6% the end-of-year close would have to be roughly $249, not $330.11.) Source: corpus/prices/UNH.csv (first and last rows).

Wrong consensus estimate (cascading into the wrong surprise %). Per corpus/research/GS/earnings.json, GS's Q4 2025 (period 2025-12-31, year 2025 quarter 4) consensus estimate was $12.0225 with surprisePercent 16.5315 (≈+16.5%), not $13.5000 / +3.8%. The actual EPS $14.01 is correct; the estimate value and the derived surprise % are both wrong, but they are internally consistent with each other ((14.01 - 13.50) / 13.50 ≈ +3.78%).

Wrong MS nine-month attributable net income (and the YoY % and the resulting rank). Per corpus/research/MS/financials_reported.json, MS's nine-month 2025 net income attributable to Morgan Stanley (concept us-gaap_NetIncomeLoss, label 'Net income applicable to Morgan Stanley', FY2025Q3 entry) was $12.464 billion, not $13.46B. Prior-period 9M 2024 NI of $9.676B is correctly cited as ~$9.68B, so the correct YoY is 12.464 / 9.676 - 1 ≈ +28.8%, not +39.1%. Because MS at $12.464B is fractionally below GS at $12.559B, the correct rank order for the bottom of the table is GS at #4 and MS at #5 — the report inverts this.
